steps.title: Match both variables
steps.content: The conclusion says floral damage affects time spent per flower differently depending on both floral morph and pollinator genus. Supporting data must compare damaged flowers with intact flowers and show that the damage effect is not the same for pin and thrum flowers or for every pollinator genus. Any option that discusses only one morph, only one genus, or only intact flowers would be too narrow.

steps.title: Check the data pattern
steps.content: Choice B compares only damaged flowers for two genera, so it does not show how damage changed visiting time. Choice C discusses only thrum flowers and misstates the pattern by saying three genera decreased. Choice D gives intact-flower times for only Habropoda. The remaining statement shows that damaged pin flowers had longer visits across all genera, while damaged thrum flowers had longer visits only for Habropoda and Osmia. That directly supports variation by both morph and genus. The answer is A.
